# WE HODL BTC

> Free Bitcoin self-custody guides and The Bitcoin Observatory - live blockchain analytics powered by a full Bitcoin Core node.

## About

WE HODL BTC is a Bitcoin education and analytics platform. It provides:
- Step-by-step self-custody guides (beginner to advanced: mobile wallets, hardware wallets, multisig)
- The Bitcoin Observatory: a live blockchain analytics dashboard with 30+ charts

## The Bitcoin Observatory

Real-time Bitcoin blockchain analytics at https://www.wehodlbtc.com/observatory

### Live Dashboard
Block height, mining difficulty, network hashrate, mempool stats, BTC price, circulating supply, UTXO count, halving countdown with measured block time estimate, and difficulty adjustment predictor.

### Network Charts
Block size, weight utilization, transaction count, TPS, block intervals, chain size growth, block fullness distribution, block time distribution, rapid consecutive blocks, weekday activity patterns. SegWit adoption, Taproot outputs, witness versions, address type evolution, address type share, witness data share, Taproot spend types, Taproot adoption velocity, cumulative SegWit/Taproot adoption, P2PKH sunset tracker, multi-type adoption velocity, UTXO flow, UTXO growth rate, transaction density, RBF adoption, batching trends, transaction type evolution (legacy/SegWit/Taproot).

### Fee Charts
Total fees per block in BTC and satoshis, average fee per transaction, median fee rate, fee rate bands (p10/p25/median/p75/p90), subsidy vs fees, fee revenue share (% of block reward), BTC transferred volume, input vs output value flow, fee pressure vs block space scatter, fee spike detector, halving era comparison, max transaction fee, protocol fee revenue breakdown (inscriptions vs Runes vs other).

### Mining Charts
Difficulty history, difficulty ribbon (7 MAs for miner capitulation detection), mining pool dominance distribution (Foundry, AntPool, ViaBTC, F2Pool, MARA, OCEAN with template miner identification, and 25+ others), mining diversity index (HHI gauge), empty block tracking by month, empty blocks by pool.

### Embedded Data Charts
OP_RETURN protocol tracking: Runes, Omni Layer, Counterparty, and other data carriers by count and bytes. Ordinals inscription count and block share. BRC-20 token activity. Stamps (bare multisig). Protocol dominance percentage. All embedded data as percentage of block size.

### BIP Signaling Tracker
Per-block miner signaling for active Bitcoin Improvement Proposals:
- BIP-110: Relaxed OP_RETURN data limits (version bit 4, 55% activation threshold)
- BIP-54: Great Consensus Cleanup (coinbase locktime == height-1, 95% threshold)
Block grid visualization, historical period chart, progress tracking per 2,016-block retarget period.

### Block Heartbeat
Real-time EKG-style visualization of Bitcoin network activity. Mempool transactions appear as blips on a flatline, block arrivals trigger spikes proportional to total fees. Click spikes for block details, click bricks for transaction details. Built on ZMQ and Server-Sent Events for sub-second updates.

### Stats Overview
Record-breaking blocks with 13 extreme categories (largest block, most transactions, highest fees, most Taproot spends, etc.). Range-filtered summary statistics across all time periods. Click any record to view full block details.

### Hall of Fame
A curated collection of 57+ remarkable Bitcoin blocks and transactions across five categories: Milestones, Records, Attacks and Stress Tests, Protocol Moments, and Oddities. From the genesis block to the latest halving, each entry provides verified data and historical context.

### On This Day in Bitcoin
Daily historical events from Bitcoin's history. Shows which blocks were mined on this date across all years, along with curated notable events (halvings, protocol activations, price milestones).

### Protocol Guide
Technical comparison of Bitcoin data embedding protocols: Omni Layer (2013), Counterparty (2014), Stamps (2023), Ordinals/Inscriptions (2023), BRC-20 (2023), and Runes (2024). Includes chronological timeline, how each works, pruning characteristics, and trade-offs.

## Self-Custody Guides

### Basic Guide
Set up a mobile or desktop Bitcoin wallet. Covers Blue Wallet, Green Wallet, and Sparrow Wallet with step-by-step instructions.

### Intermediate Guide
Set up a Coldcard hardware wallet connected to your own Bitcoin node (Start9, MyNode, or RaspiBlitz) for enhanced security and privacy.

### Advanced Guide
Create a 2-of-3 multisig wallet with multiple signing devices, steel seed backups, and geographic separation for maximum security.

## Data Source
All blockchain data is sourced directly from a full Bitcoin Core node with transaction index enabled (txindex=1). Data covers the full chain from the genesis block (January 3, 2009) to the present, updated every 60 seconds.
